name: The crown of Pallars
description:
On this Route we offer you the opportunity to test your resistance by climbing practically unseen dream roads in the Catalan Pyrenees, visiting beautiful villages perched on the mountain and visiting magical places such as Lake Montcortés or the Collegats gorge.

The figures are impressive, close to 132 kilometers and more than 2,500 meters of positive slope. If you are a cyclist with many accumulated kilometers and experience on your resume, you know that you will take advantage of every pedal stroke of this adventure, both in the sports section and on the stage.

The itinerary is circular from the La Pobla de Segur train station. As soon as you start rolling you head towards the Noguera Pallaresa course and, instead of entering the tunnels of the Collegats gorge, you take the external alternatives of the old road. This detour allows you to contemplate the cliffs from the bottom of the ravine and pass through the Argentería gorge, a limestone wall shaped by a water source that served as inspiration for Gaudí to shape his architecture.

As soon as you pass Montardit de Baix you turn to the right to ascend a slight band of asphalt that climbs twistingly from Savarneda to Puigforniu, continue to Soriguera and take the Pyrenean axis to crown the first ascent in Villamur. From here you descend with an incredible landscape to Sort to undertake a new climb that takes you through Rialp, Caregue, Sorre and Pujalt, among others, along a road that only the countrymen seem to know.

When you go back down to Sort, you pedal through the Collegats gorge again until just before Gerri de la Sal, where you turn right for the last ascent of the day that takes you winding up to the wonderful spot of the karst lake of Montcortés. From here you undertake a fun and scenic descent that passes through Pobleta del Bellveí and Senterada, on the Pyrenean axis road. To return to La Pobla de Segur you take an alternative that takes you to contemplate the impressive limestone ridges of Pallars. Back at the train station, your legs will surely feel tired, but your heart will be full from this epic adventure.
region_intro: Road cycling around La Pobla De Segur offers diverse landscapes, from agricultural plains and dense forests to dramatic gorges and rugged mountains. The region is characterized by the Noguera Pallaresa River, the Collegats Gorge, and the Boumort Mountains, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Roads are often quiet, leading through scenic areas and towards elevated viewpoints. This area in the Lleida Pyrenees provides a mix of challenging and moderate routes. Best road cycling routes around La Pobla De Segur The most popular road cycling route is Lake Montcortès and Collegats Gorge Loop, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the dramatic Collegats Gorge and past Lake Montcortès. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The crown of Pallars, a difficult 82.3 miles (132.4 km) path. This extensive route features significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views across the Pallars region. Local road cyclists also love the Noguera Pallaresa River Beach – Pobla de Segur loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses, a 29.6 miles (47.7 km) trail leading through the Noguera Pallaresa River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around La Pobla De Segur is defined by river valleys, dramatic gorges, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops along the river to difficult climbs into the Pyrenees. The routes in La Pobla De Segur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Pobla De Segur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are there in La Pobla De Segur?
A: La Pobla De Segur offers a diverse selection of 14 road cycling routes. These include 1 easy route, 6 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur?
A: The routes around La Pobla De Segur range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 6 moderate routes, often following river valleys, and 7 challenging routes that include significant climbs into the Pyrenees. There is also 1 easy route available.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in La Pobla De Segur?
A: The road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from scenic gorges to mountain passes, and the generally quiet roads that allow for an immersive experience in the Lleida Pyrenees.

Q: Are there road cycling routes with scenic viewpoints in La Pobla De Segur?
A: Yes, many routes offer stunning views. For panoramic vistas of the Pyrenees, consider routes that ascend to elevated points like the 'Coll de Montllobar'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1347466629/the-crown-of-pallars" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The crown of Pallars&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, features significant elevation gain and offers expansive views across the Pallars region. Additionally, the Santa Magdalena and Sant Miquel Viewpoints provide excellent perspectives of La Pobla de Segur and the Sant Antoni Reservoir.

Q: What natural features can I see along road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur?
A: Road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur often feature prominent natural landmarks. You can cycle along the Noguera Pallaresa River, through the dramatic Collegats Gorge, and past the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944642311/lake-montcortes-and-collegats-gorge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortès and Collegats Gorge Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, navigating both the gorge and the lake.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by road bike?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, some routes may pass near cultural points. La Pobla de Segur itself has a medieval old town, and the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Historic Village&lt;/a&gt; is a charming spot that can be a starting point or a stop on a longer ride. You might also encounter the Romanesque Shrine of Sant Miquel.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in La Pobla De Segur?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944642311/lake-montcortes-and-collegats-gorge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortès and Collegats Gorge Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noguera Pallaresa River Beach – Pobla de Segur loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Noguera Pallaresa River valley.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. La Pobla De Segur is known for its challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1347466629/the-crown-of-pallars" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The crown of Pallars&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 82.3-mile path with substantial climbing, offering a true test for experienced cyclists. Other difficult routes include those that venture into the Boumort Mountains.

Q: Are there easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are options for less experienced riders. The region offers moderate loops along the Noguera Pallaresa River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noguera Pallaresa River Beach – Pobla de Segur loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 29.6-mile trail with manageable elevation.

Q: Is La Pobla De Segur suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: For families, the flatter sections along the Noguera Pallaresa River or around the Sant Antoni Reservoir might be suitable, offering picturesque views on quieter roads. However, many of the named road cycling routes involve significant distances and elevation, so it's best to choose shorter, less challenging sections or dedicated family-friendly paths if available.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur?
A: While La Pobla de Segur is great for outdoor activities, road cycling routes are generally not ideal for dogs due to traffic and the pace of cycling. For activities with dogs, consider hiking trails or designated walking paths where they can safely accompany you.

Q: What are the parking options for road cyclists in La Pobla De Segur?
A: La Pobla de Segur, as a hub for outdoor activities, typically offers various parking options within the town itself. Look for public parking areas, especially near the town center or trailheads, which can serve as convenient starting points for your road cycling adventures.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur?
A: La Pobla de Segur is connected by public transport, including train and bus services, which can help you reach the area. However, specific public transport options directly to individual route trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bike carriage policies in advance.

Q: Are there places to eat or rest along the road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in La Pobla De Segur pass through or near small villages and towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel and rest. La Pobla de Segur itself offers a good selection of amenities. Planning your route with potential stops in mind will enhance your experience.

Q: What is the best time to visit La Pobla De Segur for road cycling?
A: The Lleida Pyrenees region, including La Pobla De Segur, is generally best for road cycling during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter may bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations, making some routes less accessible.
tips: The narrow river valley of the Rio Noguera bears a close resemblance to the canyon of the Virgin River in Zion NP. The mountains have a similarity to The Sentinel and throne of Sinawawa

L'Argentería is a limestone rock wall located in the Collegats gorge, on the Noguera Pallaresa river. Its main value lies in the whimsical shapes that a water spring has modeled on the wall; it is said that Antonio Gaudí himself was inspired by its shapes for his designs.

Gerri de la Sal is the town that is the head of the municipal area of Baix Pallars, in the region of Pallars Sobirà. It is one of the open sections, exposed to the sun and the wind. The name Sal comes from the salt pans that for many years were the main occupation of the town and that can still be seen to the east of the town!

Rialp is one of those beautiful towns with a traditional Pyrenean structure located on the banks of the Noguera Pallaresa. An ideal place to make a stop on the Route and enjoy its narrow cobbled streets and have a snack that brings you closer to local products.
This narrow road connects several towns that are completely removed from the tourist circuit and that preserve an intoxicating rural mountain spirit.

Lake Montcortés is not of glacial origin, but is a karst formation. Together with the surrounding mountains it forms a spectacular landscape, which is protected both by the Natura 2000 network and by the plan of areas of natural interest of the Generalitat of Catalonia.
Good tarmac, slightly hilly, few cars. More scenic than the main road

waypoints.name: Scenic Road to La Pobla de Segur
